name: Abandoned City of Prypiat
description: The ghost town left by the worst nuclear disaster of all time is being taken over by nature and urban explorers.

name: Tunnel of Love
description: This beautiful natural train tunnel is said to grant the wishes of visiting lovebirds.

name: Prypiat Amusement Park
description: This attraction was abandoned after the Chornobyl disaster.

name: Odesa Catacombs
description: The largest catacomb system in the world.

name: Ukrainian National Chornobyl Museum
description: This unassuming museum's slogan translates to “there is a limit of sadness, anxiety has no limits."

name: Darth Vader Statue
description: A monument to Lenin has been transformed into a statue of everyone's favorite black-helmeted, fool-choking Sith Lord.

name: Abandoned Avanhard Stadium of Prypiat
description: One of the most poignant ruins of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster, the local football team's stadium has been quickly reclaimed by nature.

name: Golden Gate of Kyiv
description: A reconstructed medieval gateway that was the magnificent entrance to the capital city dating back to 1037.

name: The Most Expensive Galician Restaurant
description: Get past the gatekeeper's apartment and enter an elaborate hoax of a restaurant with a Masonic feel.

name: Priest's Grotto
description: A subterranean Holocaust refuge within one of the world's longest cave systems.

name: Rodina-Mat : Mother Motherland
description: 340-foot statue built to honour the Heroes of the Soviet Union.

name: All Saints Church
description: The ruins of an abandoned church loom within a picturesque Ukranian village.

name: Under the Black Eagle Pharmacy Museum
description: Ukraine's oldest operating pharmacy now offers guests a fascinating walk through apothecary history.

name: House with Chimaeras
description: An Art Nouveau building covered in animal gargoyles and grotesques.

name: Duga 'Russian Woodpecker' Radar
description: This derelict superstructure was a very important warning system for the Soviet military.

name: Museum of the Great Patriotic War
description: One of the largest museums in Ukraine remembers the story of the German-Soviet War in iconic, brutalist style.

name: Peeing Colors in Kyiv
description: When you've got to go, you've got to go.

name: Arsenalna Metro Station
description: The deepest metro station in the world.

name: 'Monument of the Third Angel'
description: The biblical sculpture honors those who lost their lives, homes and communities to the Chernobyl disaster.

name: Giant Crossword of Lviv
description: World's largest crossword puzzle spans the side of a towerblock, its clues scattered throughout the city.

name: Pysanka Museum
description: Welcome to the world's only museum dedicated to the Ukranian Easter egg.

name: Museum of Unnecessary Things
description: A Kyiv-based repository for all manners of discarded curiosities.

name: Canteen 19
description: The dining hall that nourishes workers in Chornobyl's Exclusion Zone is also open to visitors.

name: Lysa Hora (Bald Mountain)
description: This hill is known for its connection to the occult in Slavic tradition.

name: Pidhirtsi Castle
description: One of the most valuable palace-garden complexes in the eastern borderlands—and one of the most haunted.

name: Kyiv Monastery of the Caves and Microminiature Museum
description: This 1000 year old relic-filled cave monastery and UNESCO World Heritage Site also hosts an amazing micro-miniature museum.

name: Kamianets-Podilskyi Castle
description: This Ukranian fortress is one of the best-preserved medieval castles in Europe.

name: Toilet History Museum
description: A quirky collection flush with information on commode culture from ancient times to modern day.

name: Potemkin Stairs
description: Featured in one of the most iconic scenes in cinema history.

name: Holodomor Victims Memorial
description: The haunting complex honors the millions of Ukrainians who died in the forced famine under Soviet rule.

name: Museum of Sexual Cultures of the World
description: It's the only museum of its kind in Ukraine.

name: Lychakiv Cemetery
description: Monumental sculptures guard many of the graves, transforming this necropolis into an open-air fine art museum.

name: Kyiv Crematorium
description: This oddly shaped building is a surprising Soviet-era crematorium.

name: Osokorky Metro Station
description: This underground transport hub doubles as an art gallery.

name: Sarcophagus of Prince Yaroslav the Wise
description: The medieval prince founded the first library in Ukraine at Saint Sophia's Cathedral where he was interred.

name: Synevyr Lake
description: This stunning mountain lake was named for a pair of tragic lovers whose statues stand at the center.

name: Slavutych
description: The last planned Soviet city was built for the former residents of Prypiat after the Chornobyl disaster.

name: Nicolaï Syadristy’s Micro Miniature Museum
description: One of the world’s best microminiaturist, Nicolaï Siadristy displays his lifetime of tiny creations in this museum.

name: Tatyana Markus Memorial
description: A tribute to a Ukrainian heroine who went undercover and killed several Nazi operatives.

name: Citadel Inn
description: A fortress built to pacify local uprising that had a stint as a Nazi concentration camp before becoming a five-star hotel.

name: Roshen Fountain
description: The spectacular Roshen Fountain show lights up the night sky all summer long, until it "hibernates" under the ice waiting for spring to come.

name: Kachalka
description: Kyiv's answer to Muscle Beach is a Soviet-era gym made from scrap metal.

name: Kyiv Institute of Information
description: This exquisite, alienesque piece of Soviet architecture is being threatened by modern shopping malls.

name: Lviv Statue of Liberty
description: This unique Lady Liberty is one of the only examples of the figure sitting down.

name: Fort Tarakanivskyy
description: This hidden fortress has been coined the "City of Ghosts."

name: Pip Ivan Observatory
description: Remains of a station that only operated for one year.

name: Beer Belly of Ukraine
description: A peculiarly shaped brass sculpture hidden in a courtyard in central Lviv

name: Mezhyhirya Residence
description: Marvel at the excessive luxury of the ousted Ukrainian president's former estate.

name: Salo Art Museum
description: This cheeky restaurant has a serious devotion to lard sculptures.

name: Znesinnia Park Rail Tracks
description: The largest park in Lviv is surrounded by a network of disused rail tracks.

name: Museum of Space Exploration
description: A century-old church is now a space museum with a treasure trove of Soviet space items.

name: Ovum II
description: Sculpture that doubles as a time capsule so that life can survive radiation outside Chernobyl.

name: Museum of Strategic Missile Troops
description: Last remaining nuclear missile base in Ukraine.

name: Lonsky Prison National Memorial Museum
description: This museum explores the tumultuous history of Lviv under Nazi and Soviet occupation.

name: Arkhivna Street
description: This street has over a half-dozen names, all at once.

name: 'Shukai'
description: A collection of miniature, symbolic sculptures hides in plain sight across Kyiv.

name: Art Center Kvartira
description: A beautiful art space and unusual mini-hotel hidden in the city center of Dnipro.

name: Black Grave
description: One of the largest remaining ancient burial mounds of the 10th century.

name: Trade Unions Building
description: The eerie burnt-out remains of a prominent building where 30 protestors lost their lives.

name: Mother-In-Law Bridge
description: Padlocks cover this rickety footbridge with love.

name: Odessa Cable Car
description: Built in the Soviet era, this cableway system still runs gondolas from Odessa down to Otrada Beach.

name: Jupiter Radiotechnology Factory
description: A huge Soviet-era robotics factory in the outskirts of Prypiat, now long-abandoned.

name: Progress Factory Mosaic
description: A unique Soviet mosaic preserved on the facade of a shoe factory in Lviv.

name: Monument to Orange
description: An artful memorial to 4,000 fateful oranges.

name: TV Tower Vinnytsia
description: Transmission tower outfitted with large crossbars that dampen wind-induced oscillations.

name: Geographical Center of Europe Marker
description: Geographers once declared this spot in Ukraine to be the center of Europe, although that status is disputed.

name: Tatiana Pick's Psychedelic Hallway
description: Like something out of a bad acid trip, this Ukraine hallway has been covered in hallucinatory phantasmagoria.

name: Yard of Lost Toys
description: This Ukraine alleyway has become its own little island of forgotten things.

name: One Street Museum Death Mask Collection
description: The largest collection of death masks in Ukraine.

name: Eternity Restaurant
description: Record-holding coffin moonlights as an eatery run by undertakers.
